author: Dr. Priya Shah
content:
EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT NOTE — 11/4/2026, 9:30 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
DOB: 8/12/2003 (Age 23)
Triage time: 8:55 PM | Triage acuity: ESI 3
Interpreter: Spanish phone interpreter (Language Line) used throughout.

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
'Mi cuerpo no puede mas' — 'My body cannot do any more.' Worsening fatigue, joint pain, intermittent rash, and feeling weak.

HPI:
Ms. Sandoval is a 23-year-old Spanish-speaking woman with known undifferentiated connective tissue disease / probable early SLE (per rheumatology 4/2026), Hashimoto's thyroiditis on levothyroxine, and recurrent medication interruptions due to financial barriers.

Course since 9/15/2026 PCP visit (Dr. Williams):
- Restarted HCQ via cash-pay 30-day supply on 9/15/2026.
- Hopkins rheumatology saw her urgently in late September 2026 — visit covered by financial-aid grant. Continued HCQ, considered increasing dose, deferred starting steroid-sparing agent pending lab confirmation of evolving phenotype, recommended close monitoring.
- October 2026: 30-day cash-pay HCQ supply exhausted ~10/15/2026. Attempted PAP bridge — processing pending. Patient has been off HCQ for approximately 3 weeks.
- Past week (10/28/2026 onward): one bad day, stayed home from work. Has not worked in approximately 8 days. Tried to come to Esperanza walk-in but the wait was too long; went home.

Symptoms today:
- Severe fatigue: She estimates she has slept 14-18 hours/day for the past 3 days. Difficulty getting out of bed.
- Joint pain: Diffuse, bilateral. Morning stiffness now lasts most of the day. Knees, wrists, hands, ankles all painful.
- Rash: Persistent over chest and upper back; today she also notes some pinkish patches on her thighs. Not itchy or painful.
- Weight: Mother says she has visibly lost more weight ('she is bones'). Patient does not know specific weight.
- Appetite: Very poor. Mother estimates she has eaten 'maybe one tortilla and some beans' today.
- Headache: Mild, intermittent over the past week.
- New: Mild dyspnea on exertion when climbing the stairs to her apartment (she lives on a 3rd floor walkup). Not at rest. No chest pain. No cough.
- Mood: Persistently low. PHQ-9 administered in triage = 14 (moderately severe). Denies SI/HI.
- Specifically denied tonight: high fever, syncope, focal weakness, vision changes, dark or bloody urine, foamy urine, oral ulcers, dysphagia for food (she ate the tortilla and beans without choking), severe abdominal pain.

Medications: Levothyroxine 25 mcg PO daily (continued). HCQ — not taking (out since ~10/15/2026). Tylenol 1g PRN for joint pain, estimates ~3g/day.

Allergies: NKDA.
Family Hx, Social Hx: As prior. Brought in by mother and aunt; no recent travel; non-sexual; uses public transit.

VITALS ON ARRIVAL (8:55 PM):
BP 96/58, HR 108, RR 18, T 98.4 F, SpO2 96% RA. Weight 43.8 kg (down from 46.4 on 9/15/2026; down 7.0 kg / 13.8% from baseline 50.8 kg on 1/12/2026). Height 150 cm. BMI 19.5. Pain 7/10.

PHYSICAL EXAM:
General: Thin young woman who appears exhausted and chronically ill. Sitting up with mother holding her arm. Engaged through interpreter. No acute distress at rest.
HEENT: Pale conjunctivae. Oropharynx clear, NO ulcers identified (carefully inspected hard and soft palate, tongue, buccal mucosa). Lips slightly dry. No cervical lymphadenopathy. Thyroid not enlarged.
Skin: Pink, faintly erythematous, blanching macular rash distributed over V-area of chest, upper back, and outer thighs. Not raised. No discoid lesions. No vasculitic changes. Diffuse scalp hair thinning marked; visible scalp at crown and frontal hairline. Nail beds pale; no nail-fold capillary changes to gross inspection. No livedo. Hands: dry, mildly scaly skin over knuckles bilaterally (subtle).
CV: Tachycardic, regular rhythm. No murmurs. JVP not elevated. Capillary refill 2 seconds.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, wheeze, or rub. RR 18.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No organomegaly. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. Mild bilateral synovitis of MCP, PIP, wrists. Left knee with small effusion, ROM limited by pain. Ankles tender. Grip strength reduced bilaterally (3/5 estimated). Proximal muscle strength: 4/5 hip flexion, 4/5 shoulder abduction; some give-way, possibly due to pain rather than weakness; uncertain.
Neuro: Alert, oriented x3. Cranial nerves intact. DTRs 2+ symmetrically. Sensation intact. Gait slow but steady, no ataxia.

ED LABS (drawn 9:15 PM):
- CBC: WBC 3.10 (low), Hgb 10.2 (low), MCV 86, Plt 184 (low-normal); ANC 1.6 (mildly low); lymphocytes 0.9 (low — lymphopenia)
- CMP: Na 135, K 3.5, Cl 99, CO2 22, BUN 6, Cr 0.5, Glu 78, Ca 8.4 (low-normal), Albumin 3.0 (low; down from 3.4), Total protein 6.1 (low), AST 32, ALT 26, ALP 76, Total bilirubin 0.5
- Mg 1.8 (low-normal), Phos 3.2, Lactate 1.4
- TSH 5.04 (mildly elevated)
- ESR 52 (elevated)
- CRP 2.3 (elevated)
- UA: 1+ protein, no blood, no leukocytes; protein:creatinine ratio 0.35 (mildly elevated, ref <0.2)
- Anti-dsDNA: low-positive at 24 IU/mL (ref <10); first positive in her history
- C3: 76 mg/dL (LOW; ref 90-180)
- C4: 8 mg/dL (LOW; ref 10-40)
- Pregnancy test: negative
- Troponin: <0.01
- BNP: 42
- Blood cultures: drawn, pending

IMAGING:
- Chest X-ray (PA and lateral): Lungs clear. No effusion, no infiltrate. Heart size normal. No widened mediastinum. Read by ED radiologist Dr. Patel.
- EKG: NSR at 102, no acute ST changes.

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
Acute presentation. Patient is high-risk and family was counseled about admission for inpatient evaluation; patient and mother declined admission tonight (financial concerns). After extensive discussion (Spanish, ~45 min) about risks, she elected discharge.

DISCHARGE ORDERS:
1. Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID — 30-day ED-pharmacy charity bridge.
2. Prednisone 30 mg PO daily x 7 days, then re-evaluate.
3. Calcium carbonate 500 mg BID, vitamin D 1000 IU daily, omeprazole 20 mg daily.
4. Continue levothyroxine.
5. Hold ibuprofen given proteinuria; APAP PRN.
6. ED return precautions reviewed (Spanish, written): fever, chest pain, dyspnea, severe headache, focal weakness, vision change, dark/foamy urine, leg/face swelling, oral ulcers, severe abdominal pain, worsening joint or rash.
7. Follow-up arranged by ED case manager:
   - Hopkins rheumatology (Dr. Tanaka) 11/9/2026 9 AM
   - Esperanza PCP (Dr. Williams) 11/10/2026 1 PM
   - Social work follow-up call 11/5/2026
8. Outpatient labs 11/7/2026: CBC, CMP, ESR, CRP, UPC, complements, dsDNA.

ED time: 7 hours. Interpreter time: 85 min.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Priya Shah, MD
Department of Emergency Medicine, Johns Hopkins Hospital
Date: 11/4/2026
